title: Tinea corporis

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected diagnostic testing when suspected ringworm may be confused with eczema, psoriasis, or another skin condition; no treatment content is mapped.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-selected diagnostic testing when ringworm is suspected but does not specify every legacy test.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected diagnostic testing when suspected ringworm may be confused with eczema, psoriasis, or another skin condition; no treatment content is mapped.

assessment.text: Supports ringworm, tinea, dermatophyte, circular scaly rash, body-site terminology, spread, person, animal, and contaminated-surface exposure context.
assessment.rationale: Supports ringworm or tinea terminology, annular scaly rash, itch or spread context, and animal or contact exposure.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports ringworm, tinea, dermatophyte, circular scaly rash, body-site terminology, spread, person, animal, and contaminated-surface exposure context.

exact_section.heading: At a glance, overview, types, and transmission
exact_section.locator: official page sections “At a glance” through “How it spreads”

assessment.text: Supports erythematous or differently pigmented raised scaly rings, central clearing, multiple or extensive lesions, and exudative lesions when bacterial superinfection has developed.
assessment.rationale: Supports annular, scaly, raised-border, central-clearing, extent, widespread, and secondary-infection documentation without diagnosing tinea.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports erythematous or differently pigmented raised scaly rings, central clearing, multiple or extensive lesions, and exudative lesions when bacterial superinfection has developed.

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-reviewed family history in psoriasis context and KOH or fungal-culture documentation when tinea corporis is a differential, without inferring either diagnosis.
follow_up.rationale: Supports clinician-reviewed KOH or fungal-culture documentation when tinea corporis is a differential.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed family history in psoriasis context and KOH or fungal-culture documentation when tinea corporis is a differential, without inferring either diagnosis.

source_limitations: Broad fungal-rash alias, fever or systemic negatives, mucosal or facial fields, immunocompromise, scalp involvement, rapid worsening, previous-treatment review, hygiene or dry-skin advice, topical treatment, return advice, fixed follow-up, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer tinea from appearance or recommend topical or oral antifungals, liver testing, hygiene measures, or referral.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
